name: White Nights Festival
traditions: Ballet and opera at the Mariinsky Theatre; Scarlet Sails celebration; drawbridge openings; midnight sun walks; classical music concerts.
historicalBackground: The festival celebrates St. Petersburg's unique geography at 59°N, where the sun barely sets in June. Peter the Great founded the city to embrace this phenomenon.
description: St. Petersburg's magical summer celebration during the midnight sun, featuring world-class ballet, opera, and the iconic Scarlet Sails spectacle.
